description: The repo-harness conflict fix is initialized as a standalone harness separate from the completed Telegram coding-intent refactor.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the repo-harness conflict fix.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry explains why new Telegram goals were being hijacked by an old in-repo harness.
Confirm the scope is limited to Telegram coding-task launch conflicts and does not reopen unrelated parser work.

description: The coding-task launcher can inspect an active repo harness and surface a concise summary instead of blindly resuming it.
steps: Inspect a repo with PLAN.json, PROGRESS.md, and init.sh present.
Verify the launcher-side harness inspection returns both the active state and a human-facing summary.
Confirm repos without a complete harness continue to report no conflict summary.

description: Telegram start-coding flow detects when a new goal targets a repo with an active harness and pauses for confirmation instead of auto-launching.
steps: Send a Telegram private-chat start command for a repo with an active harness in a focused routing test.
Verify nanobot creates the coding task but leaves it in waiting_user rather than starting Codex immediately.
Confirm the reply explains both the existing harness summary and the newly requested goal.

description: Conflict-state Telegram controls let the user explicitly continue the old harness or start the new goal.
steps: From a waiting_user conflict state, send 继续旧任务 and verify the shared launcher resumes the existing harness path.
From the same conflict state, send 按新任务开始 and verify the task launches with the explicit new-goal override recorded.
Confirm bare 继续 remains ambiguous in conflict mode and asks for an explicit choice.

description: Starting a new goal against an active harness uses an explicit prompt mode so Codex treats the old harness as background, not the primary task.
steps: Launch a task with the new-goal override against an active harness repo.
Inspect the generated Codex bootstrap prompt and verify it says not to continue the old unfinished features as the primary task.
Confirm the old harness summary is preserved as context while the new goal becomes the task focus.

description: Existing non-conflict launch behavior remains unchanged for repos without an active harness or when the user explicitly resumes the old one.
steps: Launch a Telegram coding task against a repo with no complete harness and verify it still auto-starts immediately.
Launch a conflict task and choose 继续旧任务; verify it follows the same shared launcher path as before.
Confirm create-only and launch-failure fallbacks still work.

description: Focused tests cover conflict detection, waiting-user reports, Telegram conflict controls, and prompt-mode generation.
steps: Run worker, reporting, router, and Telegram routing focused tests.
Verify the suite covers conflict summary extraction, explicit choice handling, and non-conflict regressions.
Confirm the tests stay scoped away from unrelated repo-wide failures.

description: README and progress notes explain how nanobot now handles active in-repo harness conflicts for new Telegram goals.
steps: Open the updated README coding-task section.
Verify it explains that active repo harnesses require explicit user confirmation before resuming or replacing work.
Confirm PROGRESS.md records the reasoning and the new control phrases.

description: The live nanobot gateway can be restarted in place and manually confirm that a repo with an active harness no longer hijacks a new Telegram goal silently.
steps: Restart the existing nanobot tmux gateway in place after the conflict fix.
Send a Telegram private-chat command that targets a repo with an active harness and a different new goal.
Verify nanobot replies with an explicit conflict confirmation flow instead of silently resuming old work.
